title: rhus coriaria f. longifolia
intro: Rhus coriaria f. longifolia, also known as the long-leaved sumac or prickly sumac, is a plant species within the sumac family (Anacardiaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its high content of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Rhus coriaria f. longifolia include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent, making it useful in treating wounds, soothing sore throats, and reducing fever. Herbal preparations of Rhus coriaria f. longifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate a range of health issues.
